claims.title: Mission boundaries are the product center
claims.body: Ardur binds agent sessions to declared missions and makes runtime decisions over tools, resources, flat runtime budgets, and delegation reservations. Mission-declared lineage_budgets are a v0.1 protocol goal, not a current runtime claim: non-empty lineage_budgets fail closed at compile/issue time until compiler/verifier support lands.

claims.title: Delegation narrows instead of widening authority
claims.body: Child sessions are intended to receive strictly narrower authority than their parents. The public evidence includes the ADR, Python tests, Go chain-audit tests, and the file-backed delegation reservation ledger; mission-declared lineage_budgets remain deferred and fail closed rather than implying unsupported enforcement.

claims.title: Lack of evidence is not treated as safe success
claims.body: When the runtime cannot observe enough evidence, Ardur must return a denial or unknown state instead of claiming a compliant result. This is part of the security model and known-limits story.

claims.title: The Phase 1 no-key bundle is the current rerunnable Claude Code MVP proof
claims.body: The fresh-user harness writes a redacted bundle that exercises source checkout setup, ARDUR.md profile creation, Claude Code protection, simulated allow/deny hook receipts, report verification, redaction checks, and explicit claim mapping. It supports a no-key local tool-boundary claim, not a live-Claude, package-release, eBPF, or universal-CLI claim.

claims.title: Phase 2 daemon/kernel capture is a bounded development proof
claims.body: The current dev branch includes a Linux eBPF process-lifecycle proof harness plus no-mutation daemon custody, preflight, peer-authorization, protocol/peer handshake, SO_PEERCRED retrieval, accepted-connection protocol, dry-run accept-loop invariant seams, and a no-privilege/no-execution launch-wrapper session-proof seam for deterministic argv/cwd digest evidence. This supports a local experimental boundary claim only: no production daemon install/start, socket listener/server/accept-loop/live enforcement, live SO_PEERCRED enforcement claim, eBPF load/attach, daemon-owned cgroups, universal CLI capture, file/network side-effect capture, cross-platform kernel capture, or production readiness claim.

claims.title: Starter media is archival, not yet a rerunnable public proof path
claims.body: The included casts show the product direction, but the repo explicitly marks them archival until verifier commands and artifact paths are public and re-recorded from this tree.

claims.title: MCEP v0.1 is the public protocol spine
claims.body: The public spec set defines the Mission-Controlled Execution Protocol shape, with explicit caveats where migrated specs still point at future public-import work.

claims.title: Deployment material is useful but not production-complete
claims.body: The repo includes Helm and Kubernetes/SPIRE material, but the status and limitations pages still keep deployment maturity bounded until full walkthroughs and security gates land.
